turns.content: Search for the latest ETSI news and fetch the first result

The top result appears to be the ETSI Press Releases page. Let me fetch it for the latest content.
The ETSI press releases page returned a 403 (access restricted). Let me fetch the most relevant news result instead — the 10th Cybersecurity Standardisation Conference highlights, which is the most recent dated item (March 12, 2026).
ETSI's website is returning 403 errors for direct page fetches (likely due to bot protection). Here's a summary of what the search results revealed about the latest ETSI news:

---

**Most Recent ETSI News (2026)**

**🔐 10th Cybersecurity Standardisation Conference** *(12 March 2026 — most recent)*
CEN, CENELEC, and ETSI, together with ENISA, successfully co-hosted the 10th Cybersecurity Standardisation Conference on 12 March 2026 in Brussels. The hybrid event brought together policymakers, industry leaders, researchers, and cybersecurity experts to discuss the evolving European cybersecurity landscape. The conference was titled "European Standardisation Supporting New Legislative Cybersecurity Landscape" and explored the Cyber Resilience Act and the way forward for European standardisation.

---

**Other Recent Highlights:**

- **AI Cybersecurity Standard** *(January 15, 2026)*: ETSI published ETSI EN 304 223, described as the first globally applicable European Standard for AI cybersecurity, establishing baseline requirements for AI models and systems.

- **Continuous Auditing Framework** *(January 27, 2026)*: ETSI announced the publication of ETSI TS 104 008, a new Technical Specification introducing a framework for Continuous Auditing-Based Conformity Assessment (CABCA) to help organisations ensure AI systems remain compliant throughout their operational lifecycle.

- **SNS4SNS Event** *(February 2026)*: The ETSI SNS4SNS Software & Standards for Smart Networks & Services event, held 2–5 February 2026, brought together policymakers, industry leaders, and open-source communities to shape the future of 6G.

Note: ETSI's website blocked direct page fetches (HTTP 403), but the search snippets provide a solid picture of their latest activity. You can visit [etsi.org/newsroom](https://www.etsi.org/newsroom) directly for the full articles.

ETSI is pleased to support the EuCNC & 6G Summit on 2-5 June 2026, taking place at FYCMA Malaga, Spain. The 2026 EuCNC & 6G Summit builds on two successful conferences in the area of telecommunications: EuCNC, in its 35th edition of a series, supported by the European Commission, and the 6G Summit, in its 8th edition, originated from the 6G Flagship programme in Finland, one of the very first in its area. Supported by the European Commission, the conference addresses various aspects of Beyond 5G/6G communications systems and networks.

ETSI - Producing globally applicable standards for ICT-enabled systems, applications & services deployed across all sectors of industry and society. Made in Europe for global use.

Save the Date! ETSI Security Conference 2026 - 19-22 October 2026 - Sophia Antipolis, France Join us for the 20th edition of the ETSI Security Conference, one of ETSI’s flagship events and a unique annual opportunity for the global security community to meet face to face, exchange with an experts' audience, and explore the most relevant topics in cybersecurity standardisation. This special anniversary edition will feature an intensive 4‑day programme bringing together speakers and attendees from government organisations, standards bodies, academia and a wide range of industry sectors. Programme Highlights The 2026 agenda will cover a broad spectrum of strategic and technical security topics, including: Global Cybersecurity Landscape Exploration of emerging trends, evolving threats and key regulatory initiatives shaping worldwide cybersecurity.

This framework helps organisations ensure their AI systems remain compliant with regulatory and technical requirements throughout their entire operational lifecycle.

January 15, 2026 Posted by ETSI COMS TEAM 13371 Hits ... Today ETSI announces the publication of its new standard, ETSI EN 304 223, that provides baseline cybersecurity requirements for AI models and systems. Building on the foundational work set out in its recent Technical Specification, it is a first globally applicable European Standard (EN) for AI cybersecurity. The EN has been extensively reviewed, and formally approved by National Standards Organisations voting, giving it a broader international scope and strengthening its authority across global markets. Today, ETSI announced at its upcoming plenary session on Integrated Sensing And Communications (ISAC) the launch of four new Work Items. These initiatives represent an important step towards shaping the next phase of ISAC, laying the foundations for future networks that integrate advanced sensing functions with communications systems.

The ETSI AI and Data Conference - Bringing AI and data together took place face-to-face on 09-11 February 2026, in ETSI premises, Sophia Antipolis, France. Focusing exclusively on AI in the past, the scope of this 3rd edition was extended to intertwine AI and Data management technologies. Standardisation Requests in support of the EU AI Act and the EU Data Act, means that European Standardisation Organisations are actively contributing to EU policy. The event provided an overview of current priorities in the AI and Data ecosystems, with a detailed discussion on how ETSI is addressing those. Agentic AI was also covered in a dedicated session, and will be addressed from political, industrial, and wider perspectives. Attendees had the opportunity to learn about standardisation around AI and contribute actively, highlight potential gaps, share ideas for new projects. He contributes to the company’s AI strategy, oversees research initiatives, and works to ensure that new AI capabilities are responsibly integrated into Amadeus products and services. One of his key missions is to track the latest technological and business trends in AI to assess how they can create value for the travel industry and for Amadeus in particular. He works closely with engineering and business teams to translate research insights into practical, scalable innovations that enhance the travel experience.
